poissolve.solvers.schrodinger module¶

class poissolve.solvers.schrodinger.SchrodingerSolver(mesh, carriers=['electron', 'hole'], fermidirac=None)¶

Bases: object

static carrier_density(psi, g, mxys, eta)¶
static carrier_density_deriv(psi, g, mxys, eta)¶
static lateral_kinetic_term(mesh, kperp, mxy)¶

Generates the lateral kinetic term for use in a Schrodinger solution.

This diagonal matrix is the discrete version of \(\frac{\hbar^2k_\perp^2}{2m}\). See Solving the Schrodinger Equation for details on the discrete form.

Parameters:
  • mesh – the Mesh on which the Schrodinger problem is defined
  • kperp – norm of the lateral wavevector
  • mxy – MidFunction of the lateral effective mass
Returns:

the lateral kinetic term as a sparse (CSC) matrix
solve(activation=1, eff_mass_average=True)¶
static solve_schrodinger_problem(mesh, z_kinetic_term, potential, lateral_kinetic_term=0, num_eigenvalues=3, psi_out=None)¶
static z_kinetic_term(mesh, mz)¶

Generates the symmetrized z kinetic energy term for use in a Schrodinger solution.

This tridiagonal matrix is the discrete, symmetrized representation of \(\frac{-\hbar^2}{2m_z}\frac{\partial^2\psi}{\partial z^2}\). See Solving the Schrodinger Equation for details on the discrete form.

Parameters:
  • mesh – the Mesh on which the Schrodinger problem is defined
  • mz – MidFunction of the effective mass along z
Returns:

the z kinetic term as a sparse (CSC) matrix
